dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Welcome back, here is your system overview
dashboard.registerAgentFirst: Please register an Agent instance before getting started
dashboard.taskExecutionStatsDescription: Task execution summary for the past 7 days
dashboard.welcomeMessage: This is a brand new system. Please register an Agent to get started

agents.title: Agent Management
agents.subtitle: Manage and monitor all rauto Agent instances
agents.deleteAgentSuccess: Agent "{name}" and its devices were deleted
agents.noAgentsDescription: Please register your first rauto Agent instance. Once connected, you can start managing network devices

devices.title: Device Management
devices.subtitle: Manage all network devices in one place
devices.noDevicesDescription: Please add your first network device. Routers, switches, and many other device types are supported

tasks.title: Task Orchestration
tasks.subtitle: Create and execute automation tasks
tasks.noTasksDescription: Create your first automation task. Supports template-based batch execution of network device commands
tasks.taskQueued: Task accepted by the Agent and queued for background execution
tasks.confirmExecuteDescription: Are you sure you want to execute task "{name}"? The task will be sent to the Agent for execution.
tasks.confirmCancelDescription: Are you sure you want to cancel task "{name}"? This action cannot be undone.
tasks.liveExecutionDescription: Track manager-side and agent-reported progress for this task
tasks.waitingForExecutionEventsDescription: Manager events already appear here. Detailed step logs will show up once the agent starts reporting them.
tasks.eventQueued: Task created in manager and waiting to execute
tasks.eventAccepted: Agent "{name}" accepted the task and queued it for background execution
tasks.eventDispatching: Manager is dispatching this task to Agent "{name}"
tasks.eventDispatchFailed: Failed to dispatch task to Agent "{name}": {error}
tasks.eventMarkedFailedFromErrorReport: Manager marked this task as failed based on the Agent error report
tasks.fallbackTaskFailureFromErrorReport: The Agent reported that task execution failed, but the final callback did not reach Manager

designer.title: Workflow / Orchestration
designer.subtitle: Use a visual flow to build transaction workflows and multi-device orchestration tasks
designer.workflowModeDescription: Use the visual canvas to organize workflow blocks and execution order, then dispatch to the target Agent.
designer.orchestrationModeDescription: Use the visual canvas to organize orchestration stages, target scope, and actions, then dispatch to the target Agent.
designer.dragToArrange: Drag nodes to arrange position and execution order
designer.flowCanvasDescription: Drag nodes to reposition them. Connect nodes to define the execution sequence.
designer.inspectorDescription: Edit the selected block or stage here.
designer.previewDescription: This is the exact payload that will be sent to the current Agent.
designer.noSelectionDescription: Select a workflow block or orchestration stage to edit its configuration.
designer.noConnectionRequiredDescription: Orchestration resolves targets from inventory, target groups, or inline targets.
designer.linearFlowHint: This MVP uses a linear chain. If multiple nodes branch from one node, dispatch order follows the connected chain first, then canvas position.
designer.settingsCompactHint: Compact layout is enabled here. Scroll to see the rest of the settings.
designer.settingsGroupTargetDescription: Choose the target Agent and provide the connection or target scope used during execution.
designer.settingsGroupRuntimeDescription: Control dry-run and recording so you decide how the task runs and how much execution detail is preserved.
designer.settingsGroupMetadataDescription: Maintain the workflow or orchestration name, failure strategy, and inventory-related information.
designer.previewUnavailable: Preview unavailable until the current configuration becomes valid.

history.title: Execution History
history.subtitle: View all task execution records and logs
history.searchPlaceholder: Search by task, command, or output...
history.latestRecordsDescription: Search and narrow down execution records from all completed task callbacks
history.noFilteredRecordsDescription: Try adjusting your search keywords or filters to find matching execution history
history.recordDetailsDescription: Inspect the structured result, command echoes, and related context for this execution record
history.outputUnavailable: No output captured for this execution record
history.noRecordsDescription: Once you start executing tasks, all execution records and logs will appear here

search.title: Global Search
search.subtitle: Search agents, devices, tasks, and execution history from one place
search.searchPlaceholder: Search by agent name, device host, task name, or command...
search.emptyDescription: Try an agent name, device host, task name, or command output
search.minLengthDescription: Enter at least {count} characters to start searching
search.noResultsDescription: Try a different keyword or search by host, task name, or command content

auth.adminLoginDescription: Please enter your admin account credentials
auth.footer: Rauto Manager - Network Device Automation Platform
auth.setupSubtitle: First-time use requires creating an admin account
auth.createAdminDescription: Set your admin username and password. This account will be used to log into the system
auth.setupFooter: After creating the account, you will be automatically logged into the system
auth.usernameMinLength: Username must be at least 3 characters
auth.passwordMinLength: Password must be at least 6 characters
auth.provideCurrentAndNewPassword: Please provide current password and new password
auth.newPasswordMinLength: New password must be at least 6 characters

settings.title: System Settings
settings.subtitle: Manage system configuration and preferences
settings.userProfileDescription: Manage account information and security settings
settings.emailDescription: Email address associated with the account
settings.changePasswordHint: Enter your current password and new password. New password must be at least 6 characters, including letters and numbers.
settings.newPasswordPlaceholder: At least 6 characters, including letters and numbers
settings.passwordIncomplete: Please fill in all password fields
settings.passwordMinLength: New password must be at least 6 characters
settings.passwordRequiresLetterAndNumber: New password must contain letters and numbers
settings.passwordSameAsOld: New password cannot be the same as current password
settings.agentConfigDescription: System name, Agent heartbeat, and timeout settings
settings.systemNameDescription: System name displayed in the interface
settings.agentTimeoutDescription: Timeout threshold for marking Agent offline after no heartbeat (30-600 seconds)
settings.heartbeatRangeError: Heartbeat interval must be between 5-300 seconds
settings.agentTimeoutAlertDescription: Send alert when Agent heartbeat times out
settings.deviceReportDescription: Notify when Agent reports device information
settings.taskDispatchedDescription: Notify when task is dispatched to Agent
settings.taskFailedDescription: Send alert when task execution fails
settings.appearanceDescription: Customize the light/dark mode and color scheme
settings.dataManagementDescription: View system data statistics and cleanup options
settings.clearHistoryDescription: Delete all task execution records ({count} total)
settings.confirmClearHistoryDescription: This will delete all {count} execution history records. This action cannot be undone.
settings.confirmClearNotificationsDescription: This will delete all {count} notification records. This action cannot be undone.
settings.resetConfirmationRequired: Please type "RESET" to confirm this action
settings.dangerZoneDescription: The following actions are irreversible. Please proceed with caution
settings.resetSystemDescription: Clear all business data (Agents, devices, tasks, history, notifications, configs) while keeping the admin account
settings.confirmResetDescription: This will clear all business data including Agents, devices, tasks, execution history, notifications, and system configs. The admin account will be preserved. This action cannot be undone!
settings.languageDescription: Choose the display language for the interface
settings.languageReloadNotice: The page will refresh to apply the new language

dialogs.createTaskDescription: Select dispatch type and target device, configure task parameters, then dispatch to Agent for execution
dialogs.noAvailableConnections: No available connections for this Agent
dialogs.recordLevelBasicDescription: Enable recording when you need command echoes or richer execution details in Manager.
dialogs.recordLevelTxDescription: Transaction and orchestration tasks default to Key Events so Manager can show command echoes and structured execution results.
dialogs.invalidProfileMode: Profile "{profile}" does not support mode "{mode}". The default mode is "{defaultMode}" and the available modes are: {availableModes}
dialogs.taskDetailHistoryRecordingDisabled: Command echoes were not captured because recording was disabled for this task.
dialogs.taskDetailHistoryRecordingUnavailable: Recording was enabled for this task, but the Agent did not return any command echo data.
dialogs.taskDetailRecordingDisabledDescription: This task was dispatched without recording enabled, so Manager can only show the summary result and not per-command echoes.
dialogs.taskDetailRecordingUnavailableTitle: No command echo data was returned
dialogs.taskDetailRecordingUnavailableDescriptio: This task was dispatched with recording enabled, but the Agent did not return any command echo data to Manager.
dialogs.taskDetailRecordingUnknownDescription: This task does not carry explicit recording settings. If you need per-command echoes, dispatch a new task with recording enabled.

taskResult.noCommandEchoes: No command echo data was captured for this execution

dialogs.agentDetailDescription: Inspect runtime details and devices for Agent "{name}"
dialogs.agentDetailNoDevices: No devices linked to this Agent
dialogs.agentDeleteDescription: Are you sure you want to delete offline Agent "{name}"? All devices managed by this Agent will also be deleted. This action cannot be undone.
dialogs.deviceDetailNoMetadata: No metadata captured for this device
dialogs.deviceDeleteDescription: Are you sure you want to delete device "{name}"? This action cannot be undone.
dialogs.registerAgentDescription: Run one of the commands below on your server to register an Agent. Choose HTTP or gRPC based on how the manager is deployed.
dialogs.registerAgentHttpDescription: Best when the manager only exposes HTTP(S) endpoints.
dialogs.registerAgentGrpcDescription: Best when the manager is self-hosted with a gRPC listener and the Agent cannot be reached back over HTTP.
dialogs.managerUrlDescription: HTTP mode uses the manager web URL. gRPC mode should point at the manager's gRPC listener address.
dialogs.reportModeDescription: Choose how the Agent reports back to the Manager. Use "http" for standard web deployments and "grpc" for self-hosted gRPC listeners.
dialogs.agentTokenDescription: Agent authentication token (must match Manager API Key)
dialogs.httpModeNote: The HTTP command defaults to the current browser origin.
dialogs.grpcModeNote: Set NEXT_PUBLIC_MANAGER_GRPC_URL if the manager gRPC listener uses a different public address.
dialogs.step1: Ensure rauto CLI tool is installed
dialogs.step2: Copy the command above to the server terminal
dialogs.step3: Modify the --agent-name parameter as needed (hostname recommended)
dialogs.step4: Execute the command to start the Agent
dialogs.step5: The Agent will start registration and heartbeat reporting over the selected transport
dialogs.needHelp: Need help? Check the documentation for more
dialogs.addDeviceDescription: Configure device connection information and test connection (device will be synced to rauto Agent)
dialogs.deviceNameHint: This name will be used as the connection name in rauto
dialogs.noDeviceProfiles: No Device Profiles available for this Agent
dialogs.deviceProfileHint: Dynamically fetched from the selected Agent's supported Device Profiles
dialogs.sshSecurityHint: Choose key exchange algorithm level. Secure offers highest security but may not work with older devices. Legacy Compatible supports older devices but with lower security
dialogs.passwordStorageHint: Password will be saved to rauto Agent. Manager does not store passwords
dialogs.testConnectionSuccess: Connection test succeeded! Device is reachable
dialogs.testRequiredFields: Please fill in required fields: Agent, host address, username, Device Profile
dialogs.addDeviceSuccess: Device added and synced to rauto successfully
dialogs.requiredFieldsIncludingPassword: Please fill in all required fields (including password)

taskForms.execCommandHint: Single command to execute on the device
taskForms.enterCommand: Please enter a command to execute
taskForms.templateVariablesHint: Variables used in the template, in JSON format
taskForms.templateVariablesInvalidJson: Template variables are not valid JSON
taskForms.profileModeAutoHint: When no explicit mode is sent, the Agent will use the current Device Profile default mode.
taskForms.profileModeLoading: Loading the supported modes for the current Device Profile...
taskForms.profileModeSelectConnectionHint: Select a device connection first so Manager can load the supported modes from its Device Profile.
taskForms.profileModeNoProfileHint: The selected connection does not declare a Device Profile. Keeping Auto is recommended.
taskForms.profileModeDefaultHint: This connection uses the "{profile}" profile. Its default mode is "{defaultMode}". Keeping Auto will use that default.
taskForms.profileModeAgentDefaultHint: gRPC Agents do not expose profile mode lists yet. Keeping Auto lets the Agent use the default mode from the "{profile}" profile.
taskForms.profileModeUnavailableHint: Manager could not load the supported modes for the "{profile}" profile. Keeping Auto is recommended.
taskForms.orchestrateActionProfileModeHint: When the action uses tx_block, prefer omitting the mode field so the Agent can use the target profile default mode.
taskForms.txBlockTemplateHint: Optional. Render this template first, then append any commands from the command list below.
taskForms.txBlockVarsHint: Optional JSON variables used when rendering the tx block template
taskForms.txBlockVarsInvalidJson: Template variables must be valid JSON
taskForms.txBlockRollbackOnFailureHint: Mark generated steps to roll back immediately if a step fails
taskForms.txBlockResourceRollbackRequired: Please enter a whole-resource rollback command
taskForms.txBlockRollbackTriggerRequiresResourceRo: Trigger step index requires a whole-resource rollback command
taskForms.txBlockRollbackConflict: Use either per-step rollback commands or a whole-resource rollback command
taskForms.txBlockCommandsHint: Optional additional commands. These are appended after the rendered template output.
taskForms.txBlockRollbackCommandsPlaceholder: One rollback command per line. Blank lines are allowed to preserve step alignment.
taskForms.txBlockRollbackCommandsHint: Used only for per-step rollback. When using a template, lines map to the fully resolved command order.
taskForms.txBlockNeedsTemplateOrCommands: Please provide at least one command or a template
taskForms.addAtLeastOneCommand: Please add at least one command
taskForms.workflowFailFastHint: Stop later blocks after the first failed block
taskForms.workflowJsonPlaceholder: Workflow JSON with name, fail_fast and blocks
taskForms.blockFailFastHint: Stop later steps in this block after a failure
taskForms.wholeResourceUndoRequired: Block {number} requires a whole-resource undo command
taskForms.rollbackOnFailureHint: If enabled, the failed step itself is also rolled back
taskForms.workflowJsonHint: Directly edit workflow JSON containing blocks array
taskForms.addAtLeastOneBlock: Please add at least one Block
taskForms.blockNeedsStep: Block {number} needs at least one step
taskForms.blockStepCommandEmpty: Block {blockNumber} step {stepNumber} command cannot be empty
taskForms.planFailFastHint: Stop later stages when a fail-fast stage fails
taskForms.inventoryJsonPlaceholder: Inventory JSON with defaults and groups
taskForms.inventoryJsonHint: Optional inline inventory object. Use this for groups/defaults without an inventory file.
taskForms.invalidInventoryJson: Inventory JSON must be a valid object
taskForms.stageFailFastHint: Override whether this stage stops on the first failure
taskForms.targetsJsonPlaceholder: Target array of connection names or detailed target objects
taskForms.targetsJsonHint: Optional JSON array of connection names or detailed target objects
taskForms.actionJsonPlaceholder: Action JSON, for example workflow_file or inline workflow payload
taskForms.actionJsonHint: Provide the full action object without the top-level kind field
taskForms.orchestrateJsonPlaceholder: Orchestration plan JSON with name, fail_fast and stages
taskForms.orchestrateJsonHint: Directly edit orchestration plan JSON containing stages array
taskForms.addAtLeastOneStage: Please add at least one Stage
taskForms.stageNeedsTargetSelector: Stage {number} must provide target_groups or targets JSON
taskForms.stageTargetsEmpty: Stage {number} target devices cannot be empty
taskForms.stageTemplateEmpty: Stage {number} template name cannot be empty
taskForms.invalidTargetsJson: Targets JSON must be a valid array
taskForms.invalidActionJson: Stage {number} action JSON must be a valid object
taskForms.orchestrateTxBlockActionNeedsCommandsOrT: Stage {number} tx_block action must provide commands or template
taskForms.orchestrateWorkflowActionRequiresExactly: Stage {number} tx_workflow action must provide exactly one of workflow_file or workflow

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.markAllReadFailed: Failed to mark all as read

docs.title: Documentation
docs.subtitle: User guides, API documentation, and best practices
docs.cardPageSubtitle: This page collects the documentation and repository entry points for the related Rauto projects.
docs.realDocsSubtitle: This page now reads the repository README files, roadmap documents, and UI screenshots directly, so it works as a real in-app documentation hub instead of a placeholder.
docs.featuredProjectsDescription: Use the cards below to open the documentation and source repositories for each project.
docs.managerCardDescription: Open the documentation and source repository for `rauto-manager`.
docs.rautoCardDescription: `rauto` provides the Agent runtime, CLI, command execution, templates, transaction blocks, workflows, and orchestration capabilities. It is the core project on the execution side.
docs.rneterCardDescription: `rneter` provides the lower-level SSH connectivity, session management, command exchange, and device communication primitives that `rauto` relies on during network automation tasks.
docs.localDocsDescription: Planning and reference docs already stored in this repository remain available as jump cards below.
docs.quickStartDescription: Learn how to install and configure Rauto Manager to get started quickly
docs.agentGuideDescription: Learn how to register, configure, and monitor rauto Agent instances
docs.deviceGuideDescription: Learn how to add network devices, configure connection parameters, and manage credentials
docs.taskGuideDescription: Master task templates, variable substitution, and batch execution methods
docs.cliReferenceDescription: Complete command reference and usage for the rauto CLI tool
docs.apiDocsDescription: Rauto Manager REST API documentation and examples
docs.historyGuideDescription: Review execution records, callbacks, error reports, and export filters from one place.
docs.coverageDescription: Quick start, product navigation, roadmap files, and screenshots are now surfaced here instead of static placeholder cards.
docs.gettingStartedDescription: If this is your first time bringing up the manager, this is the shortest reliable path to a complete working loop.
docs.startStepInstall: Install dependencies, copy `.env`, and fill in database plus auth settings.
docs.startStepMigrate: Run Prisma migrations and start the web application.
docs.startStepBootstrap: Create the initial admin account and verify you can sign in.
docs.startStepRegister: Register the first Agent and confirm heartbeat, transport mode, and health checks are working.
docs.startStepDispatch: Add a device and dispatch the first task to validate callbacks, history, and notifications.
docs.productGuidesDescription: These pages are already implemented and can be used directly for day-to-day operations and integration testing.
docs.screenshotsDescription: The documentation center reads screenshot assets directly from the repository so you can browse the product UI from inside the app.
docs.dashboardShotDescription: See active Agents, device reachability, task outcomes, and recent notifications at a glance.
docs.agentRegistrationShotDescription: Copy a ready-to-run `rauto agent` command and complete onboarding with auth already filled in.
docs.deviceOnboardingShotDescription: Choose an Agent, test connectivity, save the device, and keep manager inventory aligned with the Agent connection store.
docs.taskDispatchShotDescription: Dispatch single commands, templates, transaction blocks, workflows, and orchestration tasks from one interface.
docs.taskResultsShotDescription: Review callbacks, execution results, failure reasons, and execution history from one place.
docs.repoDocsDescription: These are real markdown files currently present in the repository and available directly inside the app.
docs.repoDocsHeroDescription: {count} local documents are currently indexed here, including product overviews and planning docs.
docs.noSummary: No summary could be extracted from this document yet, but the full file is still available.
docs.emptyOutline: No extractable outline was found for this document.
docs.moreDocsDescription: Other repository docs are available here as well.
docs.githubDescription: Browse source, issues, commits, and releases.
docs.libraryDescription: Underlying SSH connection and device interaction library.
